title: Faceted search vs no-results recovery
summary: Faceted search helps users narrow large result sets; no-results recovery handles the moment when narrowing or search leaves no useful path forward.
decisionRules: Use faceted search before failure, when users need structured narrowing.
Use no-results recovery after a search or filter combination produces zero matches.
Design both together when filters can remove every result.
failureModes: Adding facets without a visible way to clear applied filters.
Showing a zero-result state that does not preserve or explain the user's constraints.

sources.note: GOV.UK publishing guidance frames search filters as facets for drilling deeper into result sets.
MOJ filter guidance documents selected filters, clear filters, and applying filters to lists and search results.
Carbon filtering guidance distinguishes multi-category filtering, instant versus batch updates, and filter reset behavior.
Maersk guidance describes no-results causes and recovery actions after search or filters reach a dead end.
GOV.UK patterns emphasize task completion and recovery rather than visual-only filtering controls.
